food_subgroup: Hot coffee beverages with plant-based milk and palm sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific entry for oat milk coffee with gula melaka (palm sugar syrup). FOODON_03301236 (coffee beverage) is the closest defensible parent class. Oat milk and gula melaka components are not individually coded at this level.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Coffee, prepared) is the closest defensible parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for oat-milk-based kopitiam coffee with palm sugar sweetener.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 (Coffee) is the closest defensible substance anchor. The composite beverage with oat milk and gula melaka has no dedicated SNOMED CT concept.

preparation_method: sweetened with gula melaka (palm sugar syrup)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard takeaway cup of approximately 250 ml, as served at Toast Box and similar Singaporean kopitiam chains.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: oat milk (USDA FoodData Central NDB 2346399, Oat milk, plain, ready-to-drink) and brewed robusta coffee (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 14209) and gula melaka palm sugar (FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table, palm sugar entry), combined in proportions typical of a 250 ml kopitiam oat milk coffee with palm sugar sweetener.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central; FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Oat Milk Kopi Melaka exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Values are estimated from a weighted blend of: oat milk (~180 ml, ~60 g per 100 ml density contribution), brewed robusta coffee (~60 ml), and gula melaka syrup (~20 g dissolved). Per-100g values are derived from this composite. Energy, macros, and sodium are estimated; micronutrients not reliably derivable from component blend are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of oat milk and palm sugar components: oat milk GI ~30 (Wolever et al., 2003, Am J Clin Nutr); palm sugar / gula melaka GI ~35 (Tay et al., 2015, Asia Pac J Clin Nutr); brewed coffee GI negligible. Composite GI estimated at ~45 (low category) weighted by carbohydrate contribution of each component.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Asia Pacific Journal of Clinical Nutrition; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Oat Milk Kopi Melaka as a composite beverage. GI is estimated from the weighted carbohydrate contributions of oat milk (GI ~30, University of Sydney GI Database / Wolever et al.) and gula melaka palm sugar (GI ~35, Tay et al. 2015, Asia Pac J Clin Nutr). Brewed robusta coffee contributes negligible carbohydrate and is excluded from GI weighting. Composite GI estimated at 45 (low). Glycemic load per 250 ml serving = (45 × 19.5 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 8.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~70 mg per 250 ml serving) and absence of added salt
hypertension.guidance: Oat Milk Kopi Melaka contains approximately 70 mg sodium per serving, which is low relative to daily limits of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension may consume it occasionally but should monitor total daily sodium intake from all dietary sources.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added gula melaka (palm sugar) contributes approximately 13 g of sugar per 250 ml serving despite a low composite GI of ~45
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the composite glycemic index is low (~45) due to oat milk and palm sugar, the gula melaka adds approximately 13 g of free sugars per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, request reduced gula melaka, and account for this beverage within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content (~0.25 g per serving) and dairy-free formulation with oat milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Oat Milk Kopi Melaka is low in saturated fat (~0.25 g per 250 ml serving) and cholesterol-free, making it a more cardiovascular-friendly alternative to traditional kopi prepared with full-fat condensed milk; beta-glucan from oat milk may offer a modest LDL-lowering benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Oat Milk Kopi Melaka is a Singaporean kopitiam-style hot coffee beverage made with robusta coffee brewed in the traditional sock-filter method, combined with unsweetened oat milk and sweetened with gula melaka, the dark, aromatic palm sugar syrup originating from Malacca (Melaka), Malaysia. It is a modern plant-based adaptation of the classic Singaporean kopi, replacing the traditional full-fat evaporated or condensed milk with oat milk to create a dairy-free, lower-saturated-fat alternative. The beverage has a rich, slightly bitter coffee base characteristic of robusta beans, balanced by the caramel-like, earthy sweetness of gula melaka and the creamy, mildly oaty texture of oat milk. It is served hot in a ceramic cup or takeaway cup, typically at approximately 250 ml per serving. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 42 kcal, 0.6 g protein, 0.9 g fat, and 7.8 g carbohydrate, with a moderate sugar content of 5.2 g driven by the gula melaka component. The composite glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low category), making it a lower-GI option compared to traditional sweetened kopi. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is dairy-free and halal-friendly. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate intake due to the free sugar contribution from gula melaka, while those managing cardiovascular disease may find it a preferable alternative to condensed-milk-based kopi due to its low saturated fat content. It is widely available at Toast Box outlets across Singapore and is part of a growing trend of plant-based kopitiam beverages.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for Oat Milk Kopi Melaka exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted component blend of oat milk (USDA FoodData Central), brewed robusta coffee (USDA SR Legacy), and gula melaka palm sugar (FAO/INFOODS SEA). GI is estimated from analogous component GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis and absence of a direct composite food entry in any approved primary source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component analysis (oat milk, brewed robusta coffee, gula melaka) using USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA as approved sources. No direct composite entry exists in any approved primary source. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero by product formulation of oat milk and absence of hydrogenated fats in gula melaka and brewed coffee). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero as all components are plant-based). 3. Sentinel values (-1.0): vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the approved source databases used for estimation. 4. glycemic_index set to 45 (low category) as an analogous estimate weighted from oat milk GI (~30, University of Sydney GI Database) and gula melaka palm sugar GI (~35, Tay et al. 2015, Asia Pac J Clin Nutr); no direct GI measurement exists for this composite beverage. 5. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type throughout as no exact ontology entry exists for this specific composite beverage. 6. Allergen note: oat milk contains oats, which are a gluten-containing grain; individuals with coeliac disease or gluten sensitivity should exercise caution. 7. Price sourced from field survey data listing for Toast Box - Funan at SGD 5.60 (preserved from input record). 8. This record is served by 1 restaurant (Toast Box - Funan) per the input seed record.
